rulings.Case Title: TERI TWARKINS KELLEY ET AL VS. JANICE MARIE DEMOCKER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion And Notice Of Hearing Re Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law and Motion calendar for Friday, January 24, 2025 Line 3. PETITIONER TERI KELLEY, MORGAN STANLEY SMITH BARNEY, LLC.'s Motion And Notice Of Hearing Re Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award. Petitioners TERI KELLEY and MORGAN STANLEY SMITH BARNEY, LLC's unopposed Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award is GRANTED. Good cause appearing, the Petition to Confirm the Arbitrator's Award is granted and the arbitration is confirmed in its entirety and Judgment shall be entered consistent with the Award. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: TALIA KENNEDY VS. META PLATFORMS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $3,560.00 For Metas Withholding Privilege Log; Declaration Of William Reed; Exhibits
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery calendar for Friday, January 24, 2025 Line 1. PLAINTIFF TALIA KENNEDY's Motion For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $3,560.00 For Metas Withholding Privilege Log; Declaration Of William Reed; Exhibits. The court finds exceptional circumstances support appointment of a discovery referee for all discovery purposes. (CCP 639(a)(5); CRC 3.920(c).) (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties). Plaintiff Talia Kennedy is suing Meta Platforms, Inc. and Meta supervisors for wrongful termination under FEHA. Defendants hired Plaintiff as a product manager in March of 2018, and she claims that she was discriminated against because of her gender and familial status. Despite good performance, Plaintiff alleges she was abruptly fired. Specifically, Plaintiff alleges under FEHA (1) hostile work environment, (2) retaliation, and (3) discrimination. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: SAMIRA NEBERAY VS. HAYES VALLEY III, LP, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses By Defendant Hayes Valley Iii Lp To Plaintiffs Request For Production Of Documents Set One And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Hayes Valley Iii Lp And Its Attorney
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery calendar for Friday, January 24, 2025 Line 2. 3 - PLAINTIFF SAMIRA NEBERAY's Motion To Compel Further Responses By Defendant Hayes Valley Iii Lp To Plaintiffs Request For Production Of Documents Set One And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Hayes Valley Iii Lp And Its Attorney. The prevailing party must submit a separate proposed order for each motion. Plaintiff's motion to compel further responses to Request For Production set 1 is GRANTED. The requests at issue are reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. (CCP Section 2017.010.) Plaintiff has shown Defendant Hayes Valley III, LP's responses are incomplete. Defendant Hayes Valley III, LP has not demonstrated that any objection has merit. The court orders Defendant Hayes Valley III, LP to provide further responses to requests for production set 1 Nos. 2 through 8, 24, 29, 34, 35, 37, 38, and 40. Plaintiff's meet and confer was reasonable. Defendant Hayes Valley III, LP must provide a verification. For the responses to which Defendant Hayes Valley III, LP is claiming a privilege, Defendant shall produce a privilege log. Further responses, verification (if not yet provided) and privilege log due no later than 10 court days from notice of this order. The court does not find Defendant Hayes Valley III, LP acted with substantial justification. Defendant Hayes Valley III, LP shall pay $900 to Plaintiff in sanctions, payment no later than 10 court days from notice of this order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address.=(302.JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: SAMIRA NEBERAY VS. HAYES VALLEY III, LP, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses By Defendant Hayes Valley Iii Lp To Plaintiffs Special Interrogatories Set One And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Hayes Valley Iii Lp And Its Attorney
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery calendar for Friday, January 24, 2025 Line 2. 2 - PLAINTIFF SAMIRA NEBERAY's Motion To Compel Further Responses By Defendant Hayes Valley Iii Lp To Plaintiffs Special Interrogatories Set One And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Hayes Valley Iii Lp And Its Attorney. The prevailing party must submit a separate proposed order for each motion. Plaintiff's motion to compel further responses to Special Interrogatories set 1 is GRANTED. The requests at issue are reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. (CCP Section 2017.010.) Plaintiff has shown Defendant Hayes Valley III, LP's responses are incomplete. Defendant Hayes Valley III, LP has not demonstrated that any objection has merit. The court orders Defendant Hayes Valley III, LP to provide further responses to special interrogatories set 1 Nos. 1, 9 through 53. Plaintiff's meet and confer was reasonable. Defendant Hayes Valley III, LP must provide a verification. For the responses to which Defendant Hayes Valley III, LP is claiming a privilege, Defendant shall produce a privilege log. Further responses, verification (if not yet provided) and privilege log due no later than 10 court days from notice of this order. The court does not find Defendant Hayes Valley III, LP acted with substantial justification. Defendant Hayes Valley III, LP shall pay $900 to Plaintiff in sanctions, payment no later than 10 court days from notice of this order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ).

rulings.Case Title: JONATHAN BIKOFF VS. LIONTREE, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To File Documents Under Seal In Support Of Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law and Motion calendar for Friday, January 24, 2025 Line 9. DEFENDANTs NICHOLAS TUOSTO, BEN BRAUN,and LIONTREE, LLC.'s Motion To File Documents Under Seal In Support Of Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication. Defendants Liontree LLC, Nicholas Tuosto and Ben Braun's motion to file documents under seal, which is unopposed, is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties). For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. rulings.Case Title: ARTURO OSIEL VALENCIA BARRERA VS. DOMINO'S
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Action With Prejudice And For Entry Of Judgment Thereon
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law and Motion calendar for Friday, January 24, 2025 Line 10. DEFENDANT AMI FOODS, INC.'s Motion To Dismiss Action With Prejudice And For Entry Of Judgment Thereon. Defendant AMI FOODS, INC.'s Motion To Dismiss Action With Prejudice And For Entry Of Judgment, which is unopposed, is GRANTED. Good cause appearing, the action is ordered dismissed with prejudice and judgment shall be entered consistent with the order of dismissal. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom.
